The main claim is: Christiano agrees with Yudkowsky on the core danger: powerful AI has a good chance of deliberately and irreversibly disempowering humanity, plausibly via an abrupt 'coup,' with no reliable fire alarm beforehand.
But he disagrees with Yudkowsky's near-certainty of doom and many specific claims, holding that the situation is far more uncertain and that some alignment work is more useful than Eliezer credits.
